dublinCore.dc:title: 117 HR 6005 IH: Molly R. Loomis Research for Descendants of Toxic Exposed Veterans Act of 2025
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mrs. Dingell introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Veterans' Affairs

form.official-title: To amend the Sergeant First Class Heath Robinson Honoring our Promise to Address Comprehensive Toxics Act of 2022 to direct the Interagency Working Group on Toxic Exposure to conduct research on the diagnosis and treatment of health conditions of descendants of individuals exposed to toxic substances while serving as members of the Armed Forces, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Molly R. Loomis Research for Descendants of Toxic Exposed Veterans Act of 2025.

section.header: Research on diagnosis and treatment of health conditions of descendants of individuals exposed to toxic substances while serving in the Armed Forces

subsection.header: Interagency Working Group on Toxic Exposure Research

paragraph.header: Authority to establish interagency task forces
paragraph.text: Subsection (b) of section 501 of the Sergeant First Class Heath Robinson Honoring our Promise to Address Comprehensive Toxics Act of 2022 (Public Law 117–168) is amended by adding at the end the following new paragraph:
Establish Federal interagency task forces to conduct collaborative research activities.
Subsection (c) of such section is amended by striking paragraph (3) and inserting the following new paragraphs:
Not later than one year after the date of the enactment of the Molly R. Loomis Research for Descendants of Toxic Exposed Veterans Act of 2025—

subparagraph.text: the findings of the members of the Working Group with respect to the collaborative research activities described in paragraph (2);
the strategic plan developed, by the Working Group under subsection (b); and
such recommendations as the Working Group may have for legislative or administrative action to improve collaborative research activities among the members of the Working Group.

paragraph.text: Not less frequently than annually during the five-year period covered by the strategic plan developed under subsection (b)—

subparagraph.text: a summary of the collaborative research activities carried out by the members of the Working Group and the findings of the members with respect to such research activities;
a progress report on implementation of the strategic plan developed by the Working Group under subsection (b); and
such recommendations as the Working Group may have for legislative or administrative action to improve collaborative research activities among members of the Working Group.
by redesignating subsections (c) through (e) as subsections (d) through (f), respectively;
in subsection (e), as redesignated by paragraph (1), by striking under subsection (c) and inserting under subsection (d); and
by inserting after subsection (b) the following new subsection (c):

subsection.header: Requirements relating to certain research on impacts to descendants
subsection.text: The Working Group and the Agency for Toxic Substances and Disease Registry shall—

paragraph.text: establish an interagency task force to conduct research on the diagnosis and treatment of health conditions of descendants of toxic-exposed veteran (as defined in section 101 of title 38, United States Code);

maintain a publicly available website with information on the activities and findings of the Agency under subparagraph (A), including a review of all relevant data to determine the strength of evidence for a positive association between a health condition researched and a toxic exposure risk activity (as defined in section 1710(e)(4) of title 38, United States Code) based on the categories set forth under section 1173(c)(2) of title 38, United States Code; and
perform other actions as directed by the Secretary.

subsection.header: Study of descendants of veterans of Operation Ranch Hand

paragraph.text: Not later than, the Secretary of Veterans Affairs shall conduct a study on the biological descendants of veterans who served in Operation Ranch Hand to—

subparagraph.text: assess the rates of birth defects and developmental delays among such descendants; and

clause.text: genetic factors that prevented some of such descendants from developing such a birth defect or developmental delay;
causes of related health outcomes; and
preventative measures to mitigate risks associated with toxic exposures.

paragraph.text: Participation in the study shall be voluntary. To recruit participants, the Secretary shall contact such veterans and descendants.
To carry out the study, the Secretary shall use—

subparagraph.text: biological samples from such veterans and descendants to which the Secretary has access;
health records of such veterans and descendants to which the Secretary has access; and
surveys and questionnaires of such veterans and descendants, to identify relevant—

paragraph.text: To analyze data collected under subsection (c), the Secretary shall—

subparagraph.text: use statistical analysis (including regression models and comparative analyses) to determine correlations between toxic exposure and health outcomes; and
conduct genomic sequencing and analysis to identify genetic markers associated with health issues in such descendants.

paragraph.text: Not later than, the Secretary shall publish the findings of the study.
